meta.description: Mezzanine-UI upgrade work manifest. Consume this file to drive .md documentation updates.

changelog.name: @mezzanine-ui/react@1.1.0
changelog.body:
## `@mezzanine-ui/react@1.1.0`

> Minor release: `SelectButton` gains a new `closeOnSelect` prop and closes its dropdown on option click by default. Also fixes an SSR hydration mismatch in `Table` and restores keyboard navigation across the portalled picker family — completing the portal migration started in `1.0.4`.

---

### 🚀 Improvements

- **`SelectButton`** — New `closeOnSelect` prop (defaults to `true`) closes the dropdown after an option click, matching single-select UX expectations. The internal `Dropdown` is now controlled via `open` / `onVisibilityChange` so the behaviour is deterministic across controlled and uncontrolled call sites. Consumers who relied on the dropdown staying open after selection can opt out per call site with `closeOnSelect={false}` — a dedicated story demonstrates this.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **`Table`** — Fixes an SSR hydration mismatch that affected Next.js / Remix consumers. Row height was being resolved via `getComputedStyle` inside `useMemo` at render time, which returned `0` on the server and the real pixel value on the client, causing React to warn (and in strict mode, throw) during hydration. The CSS-variable read is now deferred to `useIsomorphicLayoutEffect` so the first render is deterministic across server and client, and the measured height is applied on the next paint.
- **`DatePicker` / `DateRangePicker` / `TimePicker` / `DateTimePicker` / `DateTimeRangePicker` / `TimeRangePicker` / `MultipleDatePicker`** — Restores `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` navigation between the trigger input and the calendar / time panel after the popper was portalled out in `1.0.4`. Because the portal severs natural DOM traversal order, the popper now establishes an explicit logical focus loop (trigger → popper → trigger). The implementation uses ref-mirrored handlers to avoid closure staleness in the document-level `keydown` listeners, plus a direct `keydown` binding on the trigger so focus cycling stays reliable inside a `Modal` focus trap.

---

**Compatibility**: requires `@mezzanine-ui/core` ≥ 1.0.4, `@mezzanine-ui/system` ≥ 1.0.2, `@mezzanine-ui/icons` ≥ 1.0.2

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mezzanine-UI/mezzanine/compare/@mezzanine-ui/react@1.0.4...@mezzanine-ui/react@1.1.0

changelog.name: @mezzanine-ui/react@1.0.4
changelog.body:
## `@mezzanine-ui/react@1.0.4`

> Fixes the entire picker family (`DatePicker`, `DateRangePicker`, `TimePicker`, `DateTimePicker`, `DateTimeRangePicker`, `TimeRangePicker`, `MultipleDatePicker`) when rendered inside a `Modal` or near the viewport bottom edge. Restores behaviour parity with `@mezzanine-ui/ng`'s picker popper.

---

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **`DatePicker` / `DateRangePicker` / `TimePicker` / `DateTimePicker` / `DateTimeRangePicker` / `TimeRangePicker` / `MultipleDatePicker`** — The popup no longer gets visually clipped when the picker sits inside a `Modal` (or any ancestor with `overflow: hidden`). The internal `InputTriggerPopper` previously hard-coded `disablePortal`, leaving the popup in the same stacking/clipping subtree as the modal body. The popper now portals out by default, so `z-index: popover (1005)` actually wins against `Modal (1004)`. Consumers who relied on the old inline rendering can opt back in per call-site with `popperProps={{ disablePortal: true }}`.
- **`DatePicker` / `DateRangePicker` / `TimePicker` / `DateTimePicker` / `DateTimeRangePicker` / `TimeRangePicker` / `MultipleDatePicker`** — The popup now flips above the trigger when there isn't enough room below. The popper was previously anchored to `bottom-start` with no flip middleware, so a picker near the viewport bottom had its popup cut off by the window edge. Floating UI's `flip` middleware is now enabled with `padding: 8` and `fallbackAxisSideDirection: 'end'` so the panel re-opens upward (or sideways) before grazing the edge.
- **`DatePicker` / `DateRangePicker`** — Internal hover-based interactions (the `onMouseLeave` overlay inside the calendar body) now receive mouse events correctly once the popup is portalled *(via `core@1.0.4`)*. The default portal container is `pointer-events: none` so the rest of the page stays interactive; children must opt back in, which was missing for the picker popper class.

### 🏗 Internal

- **`DatePicker`** — New Storybook regression stories `InsideModal` and `NearViewportEdge` guard the two fixes above. They mirror the `DrawerModalSelectLayering` story added earlier for `Select` / `Dropdown`, so every family of portalled popup now has a layering regression guard.

---

**Compatibility**: requires `@mezzanine-ui/core` ≥ 1.0.4, `@mezzanine-ui/system` ≥ 1.0.2, `@mezzanine-ui/icons` ≥ 1.0.2

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Mezzanine-UI/mezzanine/compare/@mezzanine-ui/react@1.0.3...@mezzanine-ui/react@1.0.4
